metadata.note: Eval 1 run via live delegation subagent (executor + grader). Evals 2-3 remain analytical pending Claude CLI availability.

runs.eval_name: Quran API endpoint and Sahih International translation ID

expectations.text: Response identifies Quran.com API v4 as primary
expectations.evidence: 'The primary API provider for Islam is Quran.com API v4' -- explicitly labeled as primary

expectations.text: Response provides the correct endpoint with surah:ayah key format
Response correctly identifies translation ID 20 as Sahih International
expectations.evidence: 'The translation ID for Sahih International is 20' with example URL including translations=20

expectations.text: Response mentions the AlQuran.cloud fallback
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Fallback Option' section shows AlQuran.cloud endpoint with en.sahih edition

runs.eval_name: Quran API endpoint and Sahih International translation ID

expectations.text: Response identifies Quran.com API v4 as primary
expectations.evidence: Without-skill listed AlQuran.cloud API first (as primary), Quran.com second. ARE standard is Quran.com v4 primary, AlQuran.cloud fallback only. Inverted.

expectations.text: Response provides the correct endpoint with surah:ayah key format
expectations.evidence: Correctly provides verses/by_key/{verse_key} with 2:255 colon format

expectations.text: Response correctly identifies translation ID 20 as Sahih International
expectations.evidence: Correctly states 'Sahih International Translation ID: 20' -- this is genuine LLM training data knowledge

expectations.text: Response mentions the AlQuran.cloud fallback
expectations.evidence: AlQuran.cloud mentioned as PRIMARY provider (listed first), not as a fallback. Inverted relationship leads developer to use wrong primary source.

notes: Translation ID 20 is genuine LLM knowledge -- the matrix's main value is for less-common translations. Primary/fallback ordering is the ARE-specific differentiator.

expectations.text: Response lists all four volumes of the Standard Works
Response explains the isLdsBibleRef routing decision
Response mentions scriptures.nephi.org for non-Bible Standard Works
Response mentions the graceful fallback for LDS API unavailability

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability

runs.eval_name: Orthodox Christian canon and API gap

expectations.text: Response correctly states the Orthodox canon is Septuagint-based
Response gives the book count range: 76-78 books
Response explicitly states the API gap for 3 Maccabees, Psalm 151, and 4 Maccabees
Response confirms bible-api.com web is the best available free option for Orthodox use

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability

runs.eval_name: Orthodox Christian canon and API gap

expectations.text: Response correctly states the Orthodox canon is Septuagint-based
Response gives the book count range: 76-78 books
Response explicitly states the API gap for 3 Maccabees, Psalm 151, and 4 Maccabees
Response confirms bible-api.com web is the best available free option for Orthodox use

notes: [ANALYTICAL] -- live subagent run pending Claude CLI availability
Eval 1 is a LIVE run -- without_skill scored 0.5 (not 0.25 as estimated)
Key finding: LLM correctly knew translation ID 20 = Sahih International from training data. Skill's matrix adds more value for less-common translations.
Primary/fallback ordering is the real ARE-specific differentiator for eval 1, not the translation ID
